info.name: Fitscale Backend API
info.description:
API collection for the Fitscale backend.

## Auth
- Uses httpOnly cookies (`accessToken`, `refreshToken`). Log in first; Postman sends cookies automatically on later requests to the same host.
- Tokens are **not** returned in the JSON body — only a sanitized user object (`passwordHash` and `token` are omitted).
- Refresh and logout identify the user from the `refreshToken` cookie — no email in the request body.
- Cookies use `Secure` only when `NODE_ENV=production`.

## Protected routes
- `/api/workouts` and `/api/total-workouts` require a valid `accessToken` cookie on all routes.
- `/api/users` — most routes require auth; `POST /api/users` (register-style create) is public unless middleware is added later.

## User roles
- `ADMIN` — list/delete all users; list all total-workout records via `/api/total-workouts/all`
- Owner or `ADMIN` — get/update/delete a user by `:id`
- Any authenticated user — own workouts and total-workout records only

## Workouts & totals
- **Create workout** — syncs `TotalWorkouts` inline and publishes `workout-created` to RabbitMQ (`{ workoutId, userId, workoutType }`). The total-workouts consumer also syncs from the queue.
- **Update workout** — syncs `TotalWorkouts` inline (both old and new type if changed) and publishes `workout-updated` to RabbitMQ.
- `TotalWorkouts` stores one row per `(userId, workoutType)` with aggregated `totalCount`.

## RabbitMQ queues
- `workout-created` — published on workout create
- `workout-updated` — published on workout update

## Validation
- Register/create password: min 8 characters
- User `:id` must be a UUID
- Workout `:id` must be a UUID
- Total workout `:id` must be a positive integer
- Workout `workoutType`: PUSHUP | SQUAT | SITUP | PLANK
- Workout `count`: positive integer
- Total workout `totalCount`: non-negative integer

## Base URL
- Default `{{baseUrl}}` is `http://localhost:5001`. Change the collection variable if your `PORT` in `.env` differs (e.g. `5002`).

request.description:
Health check.

**Response 200:** `{ success: true, message: "Backend running" }`

item.name: Auth
item.description: Public auth routes. Login and refresh set httpOnly cookies.

request.description:
Registers a new user (role defaults to USER).

**Body:** `{ email, password }` — password min 8 chars

**Response 201:** `{ user, success, message }` — user is sanitized (no passwordHash/token)

**Response 400:** validation or duplicate email error

Logs in a user.

**Body:** `{ email, password }`

**Sets cookies:** `accessToken` (1h), `refreshToken` (7d)

**Response 200:** `{ user, success, message }` — no tokens in body

**Response 401:** invalid credentials

Issues new tokens using the `refreshToken` cookie. No request body.

**Requires:** valid `refreshToken` cookie (from Login)

**Sets cookies:** new `accessToken` and `refreshToken`

**Response 200:** `{ user, success, message }`

**Response 401:** missing, invalid, or revoked refresh token

Logs out the current user.

**Requires:** valid `refreshToken` cookie

**Clears cookies:** `accessToken`, `refreshToken`

**Invalidates** stored refresh token in DB

**Response 200:** `{ success, message }`

**Response 401:** missing or invalid refresh token

item.name: Users
item.description:
User management routes.

- `POST /` — create user (no auth middleware on route today)
- Admin only: list all, delete all
- Owner or admin: get/update/delete by `:id`

request.description:
Creates a user.

**Body:** `{ email, password, role? }` — role: ADMIN | TRAINER | USER (defaults to USER)

**Response 201:** `{ user, success, message }`

Returns all users ordered by `createdAt` desc. **Admin only.** Requires `accessToken` cookie.

**Response 200:** `{ users, success, message }` — users are sanitized

Returns a single user. **Owner or admin.** Requires `accessToken` cookie.

**Params:** `id` — UUID

**Response 200:** `{ user, success, message }`

**Response 403:** not owner and not admin

**Response 404:** user not found

Updates a user. **Owner or admin.** Requires `accessToken` cookie. All body fields are optional.

**Body:** `{ email?, password?, role? }` — password min 8 chars if provided

**Response 200:** `{ user, success, message }`

**Response 403:** not owner and not admin

Deletes a user. **Owner or admin.** Requires `accessToken` cookie.

**Params:** `id` — UUID

**Response 200:** `{ success, message }`

**Response 403:** not owner and not admin

Deletes all non-admin users. **Admin only.** Requires `accessToken` cookie.

**Response 200:** `{ success, message }`

item.name: Workouts
item.description:
Protected routes. Require `accessToken` cookie. Workouts are scoped to the authenticated user.

**Side effects (async via RabbitMQ fanout):**
- Create/update/delete publish to `workout-events` exchange
- `total-workouts-sync` consumer updates aggregated totals
- `badge-evaluation` worker awards badges based on totals

request.description:
Creates a workout for the authenticated user.

**Body:** `{ workoutType, count }`
- `workoutType`: PUSHUP | SQUAT | SITUP | PLANK
- `count`: positive integer

**Side effects:** publishes workout event → total-workouts sync + badge evaluation

**Response 201:** `{ workout, success, message }`

Returns workouts for the authenticated user, ordered by `createdAt` desc.

**Response 200:** `{ workouts, success, message }`

Returns a single workout owned by the authenticated user.

**Params:** `id` — UUID

**Response 200:** `{ workout, success, message }`

**Response 404:** workout not found

Updates a workout by id.

**Params:** `id` — UUID (required — set `{{workoutId}}` from Create Workout)

**Body:** `{ workoutType, count }` — both required

**Side effects:** publishes workout updated event with `previousWorkoutType` if type changed

**Response 200:** `{ workout, success, message }`

**Response 404:** workout not found

Deletes a workout by id.

**Params:** `id` — UUID

**Side effects:** publishes workout deleted event → total-workouts sync + badge re-evaluation

**Response 200:** `{ success, message }`

**Response 404:** workout not found

item.name: Total Workouts
item.description:
Protected routes. Require `accessToken` cookie.

Stores aggregated workout counts per `(userId, workoutType)`. Synced automatically by the RabbitMQ consumer when workouts change. These endpoints allow reading and manual management.

- Authenticated user: own records only
- Admin: list all records via `GET /all`

request.description:
Returns all total-workout records for the authenticated user (one per `workoutType`).

**Response 200:** `{ totalWorkouts, success, message }`

Each item: `{ id, userId, workoutType, totalCount }`

Returns every total-workout record in the system. **Admin only.**

**Response 200:** `{ totalWorkouts, success, message }`

Returns a single total-workout record owned by the authenticated user.

**Params:** `id` — positive integer

**Response 200:** `{ totalWorkout, success, message }`

**Response 404:** record not found

Manually creates a total-workout record for the authenticated user.

**Body:** `{ workoutType, totalCount }`
- `workoutType`: PUSHUP | SQUAT | SITUP | PLANK
- `totalCount`: non-negative integer

**Note:** Only one record per `(userId, workoutType)` is allowed. Prefer creating/updating workouts — totals sync automatically.

**Response 201:** `{ totalWorkout, success, message }`

**Response 400:** duplicate record for this user and type

Updates a total-workout record owned by the authenticated user. All body fields are optional.

**Params:** `id` — positive integer

**Body:** `{ workoutType?, totalCount? }`
- `totalCount`: non-negative integer

**Response 200:** `{ totalWorkout, success, message }`

**Response 404:** record not found

Deletes a total-workout record owned by the authenticated user.

**Params:** `id` — positive integer

**Response 200:** `{ success, message }`

**Response 404:** record not found

item.name: Badges
item.description:
Protected routes. Require `accessToken` cookie.

Badges are awarded automatically by the badge worker when workout totals cross thresholds (100/500/1000 per type). Manual award/update/delete also available for the authenticated user.

request.description:
Returns all badges for the authenticated user.

**Response 200:** `{ badges, success, message }`

Returns a single badge owned by the authenticated user.

**Params:** `id` — integer

**Response 200:** `{ badge, success, message }`

Manually awards a badge to the authenticated user.

**Body:** `{ badgeType }` — e.g. BRONZE_PUSHUP, SILVER_SQUAT, GOLD_PLANK

**Response 201:** `{ badge, success, message }`

Deletes a badge owned by the authenticated user.

**Response 200:** `{ success, message }`
